title: EndpointType is a manual parse/display enum while Endpoint.endpoint_type stays String: correct separation of wire type from semantic type
description: Endpoint.endpoint_type is typed as String on the wire (accepting future extension URIs without deserialization failure), while EndpointType is a separate semantic enum with from_uri/as_uri conversion methods. This separation is correct: (1) if endpoint_type were serde-driven, an unrecognized URI would fail deserialization and discard the entire Endpoint object; (2) keeping the wire type as String and providing a companion enum gives callers a clean API for matching known types while preserving unknown URIs exactly. A future contributor might suggest making endpoint_type an #[serde(rename_all)] enum (like ChatKind) to unify the pattern. That would silently lose the original URI string for unknown types — EndpointType::Other(String) round-trips the raw URI, but a serde-driven #[serde(other)] variant cannot. The EndpointType::as_uri/from_uri round-trip is tested in test_endpoint_type_as_uri_round_trips.
